value.market_read: WTI Curve: WTI settled at $76.54 in the prior session, firming after the latest WPSR (week ending June 12, released Wed Jun 17) confirmed a prompt-tightening picture at Cushing that drove front-month upside into the settle. The curve is in clean backwardation across the strip, with M1 ($76.54) sitting roughly $6.54 above the M12 ($70.00), a structure consistent with near-term supply tightness rather than a storage/surplus play. The M1–M2 spread holds near $0.92 in backwardation, signaling the prompt is bid relative to deferreds and reinforcing the tight-balances read off the reported Cushing draw. Steady backwardation of this magnitude keeps roll yields positive for longs and discourages stockbuilding. Watch: Whether subsequent WPSR prints confirm continued Cushing tightness or the prompt spread begins to flatten as deferred length rebuilds. Brent Curve: Brent settled at $80.59 in the prior session, holding a premium to WTI as the global benchmark tracked the same constructive prompt tone. The curve is backwardated with M1 roughly $6.37 over the back of the strip ($74.22), a flatter slope than WTI's at the very front but broadly signaling near-term tightness in waterborne barrels. The front spreads remain in backwardation, consistent with firm prompt demand and limited incentive to store. The Brent backwardation slightly lagging WTI's prompt steepness reflects the more acute domestic Cushing pull versus the seaborne market. Watch: Whether geopolitical risk premia tied to Hormuz transit flows reassert and steepen the prompt Brent structure. RBOB Gasoline Curve: RBOB settled at $2.9122 in the prior session, with the summer driving-season pull keeping the prompt firmly bid. The curve shows steep backwardation from M1 down into the autumn contracts before the seasonal trough, with the M1–M12 spread near 42.7 c/gal — a textbook summer-gasoline shape pricing peak-season tightness now and demand decay into winter. The front backwardation of roughly 11.3 c/gal between M1 and M2 underscores how tightly the prompt is pricing summer barrels. Note the curve bottoms around the winter contracts before nudging higher into spring 2027, the usual RVP/spec transition kink. Watch: Whether refinery runs keep pace with summer product-supplied demand or draws accelerate and steepen the prompt further. ULSD Diesel Curve: ULSD settled at $3.1508 in the prior session, supported by firm distillate balances heading through summer. The curve is backwardated, sloping steadily down to $2.7935 at the back with an M1–M12 spread near 35.7 c/gal — a smoother, less seasonally-kinked decline than gasoline, reflecting structural distillate tightness rather than a single-season demand spike. The M1–M2 backwardation near 3.4 c/gal signals a modestly bid prompt without the acute urgency seen in gasoline. The lean distillate inventory backdrop carried through recent WOPUs keeps the structure firm into the back half of the year. Watch: Early signs of heating-demand positioning and any diesel/jet regrade shifts that could tighten the distillate prompt into winter.
value.kpi_wti: WTI settled at $76.54, firming into the settle as the latest WPSR reinforced tight Cushing balances at the prompt.
value.kpi_brent: Brent settled at $80.59, holding its premium to WTI while tracking the same constructive near-term tone.
value.kpi_rbob: RBOB settled at $2.9122, with summer driving-season demand keeping the prompt firmly bid and the curve in steep front backwardation.
value.kpi_ulsd: ULSD settled at $3.1508, underpinned by lean distillate inventories and a steady backwardated structure into the back half of the year.
value.kpi_bw: The Brent–WTI spread sits at a narrow $4.05, signaling weak/uneconomic US export arb — barrels stay domestic and the spread would need to re-widen (WTI falling or Brent rising) to restore export competitiveness.
value.kpi_curve_regime: Both crude benchmarks are in clean backwardation (WTI M1–M12 at $6.54, Brent at $6.37), pricing near-term supply tightness rather than surplus, with positive roll yields discouraging storage.
